Having to experience a lawsuit is something we all hope we never have to do in life. It is a very long process, stressful, taxing, and stringent. Unfortunately, in this country people are allowed to sue for anything, regardless of how you feel; once you are served you are now responsible to accept any allegations upon you.

Unless!

You reach out to Colosseum Counsel and acquire defense from James R. Chillemi, Esq.
He is knowledgeable and wise attorney who keeps his client's best interest at hand. Thanks to James my wife and I slept every night knowing we were covered during one of the more stressful times in our lives.
From the start, in the during, and through the finish we always were in the loop. James explained everything as it happened, he even took the time out of his day to reach out and check in. I couldn't be more thankful for Colosseum Counsel and James R. Chillemi.
James was a shield of light during a time when darkness was attempting to fall. Because of him, it never did.

Seldomly do my sister and I ever feel strongly enough about something that we find ourselves leaving a review, especially when that something is such a deeply personal situation, but if this finds its way onto the screen of just one person in the same hopeless place that we were in a few months ago then it was worth sharing. Vito Roppo is one of the most PHENOMENAL and passionate attorneys we have ever met (and I’m marrying one so that’s saying a lot!). If you are seeking guardianship representation then it is safe to assume that you are more than likely amidst an emotionally devastating and potentially time-sensitive crisis. That is precisely where we found ourselves about six months ago. We chased down every solution we could, contacted every advocate we could and pursued any and every lead we got in desperation to care for our mother but we hit legal roadblock after legal roadblock. Finally our determination lead us to Vito. He has treated us as a priority since the very first phone call. This man is honest, intelligent and above all passionate about what he does. Vito has devoted his legal career to understanding the complicated nuances behind this wildly misunderstood field of law. He has professional and personal experience with guardianship so you will be treated with compassion, which at times like these mean more than you will ever know. You will not feel like just another case number or client while working with him. He was with us every single step of the way for our many calls, questions, emails and appearances. Vito was a calming sense that made us realize it was all going be okay in the end. Fast forward to our final hearing when my sister and I were finally appointed our mothers legal guardians. Our mothers life has changed for the better and our entire family can now breathe a sigh of relief that we are in a position to help her in ways she couldn’t help herself. None of this would have been possible without Vito. I would recommend him 100000 times over to anyone seeking help in this area of law.
Vito, thank you for all you did for our family, we are forever grateful.
Scott T. Moorey criminal defense

reviews.text: After Hurricane Irma in 2017, our Insurance company continued to deny, and stall, our legitimate damage claims for our condominium in Naples. We filed all the necessary claim documentation required by the insurance company to the letter, along with photographs of all the interior and exterior damage. After almost a year of blatant stall tactics and boiler plate denial letters, we decided to get legal help. Weldon and Rothman were recommended to us. We contacted them, and they got right on the case. They assembled a legal team, inclusive of an Umpire, and negotiated for us with our insurance company. It didn't take too long, our insurance company settled for more money than we originally asked for on our claim. I highly recommend Brad Rothman, Esq. and the Weldon & Rothman firm for any damage claims you may have in the future. John Coughlin, Naples, FL
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you, John, for taking the time to share your experience and for your thoughtful recommendation. We know how frustrating and exhausting it can be to deal with prolonged insurance delays and denials, and we’re glad Brad Rothman and our team were able to step in, advocate on your behalf, and achieve a successful resolution.

We appreciate the trust you placed in Weldon & Rothman and are pleased that the outcome exceeded your expectations. Thank you again for your kind words. We wish you all the best.

reviews.text:
In today’s world you might be fearful of obtaining a lawyer for your case. That is a real worry, except when dealing with the folks at The Law Firm of Anidjar & Levine. From the beginning, to the conclusion of my case, I was treated with respect and felt they genuinely supported me in this process with warm, sincere, personal care.  I had the pleasure of meeting with Mark Anidjar and as I walked away from our meeting, I truly thought, “This man knows more, with his ability to communicate so completely with me as a lay person, that I truly understand how my body was reacting to my accident, better than my Drs. explained it to me!! I was very impressed with his knowledge and I tell you, the trust factor went up 100%.  That was a great, satisfying feeling.
Then as it grew closer to the completion with the negotiations of my medical bills, et al, Mr. Shire Patel, (also a lawyer at the firm), took great care of me with his administrative assistant.  They answered all of my questions as we went through the long process.  I believe he went above and beyond on my behalf.  Who does that in today’s self-absorbed world????
He answered my phone calls immediately. Mr. Patel carried me through with a wonderful, settling of my case.  The support staff also had the same commitment to the firm’s clients.  This was the best, least stressful, important thing I have ever had to be a part of. I cannot say enough positive words in their behalf.
Bravo Mr. Patel, Mr. Anidjar, Mr. Levine, and your incredible support staff!
Vicky Richmond

reviews.text:
Worst attorney for ANY PARENT!
CAUTION to ALL PARENTS: This lawyer will manipulate you, Persuade you to make costly medical decision to boost HIS bottom line, make you run around town running errands on your dime & time, then when he settles the case...HE WILL TREAT YOU like he doesn't know you & do everything to cut you out of your child's life over MONEY!

I got into an accident with my children. My eldest daughter who was also in the accident turned 18 soon after the accident. I continued to support her, feed her, clothe her, pay for her education, drive her around to appointments, pay all her expenses & perform all the tasks needed to help my daughter as I had done her whole life for nearly 2yrs until the case was settled.
I hired Mr gluck who had consulted with me the entire time & used me to boost the value of the case. He never met my daughter for the duration of the entire case, didn't even bother to send flowers to the intensive care unit where I slept in a chair watching over my daughter for a week.
Once the case was completely settled (thanks to all the footwork I did) & I had successfully put a $260,000 settlement on his lap, this SOB did a 180 turn on me (his client)  went behind my back to purposely cut me out, consulted my now 19 yr old daughter (who was now living in another state) who had never hired him or met him in her entire life, sent her the settlement & ordered her not to talk to me or give me any money.
My daughter disappeared & I have not seen her since. The icing on the cake is that he did this right at Thanksgiving. He specifically told me he wanted to make sure I didn't go "Holiday shopping" with the money.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hello, Lara. I understand your frustration for not receiving the settlement in your name but the law requires me to provide the settlement to the person injured. In this case, your daughter. If your daughter would have been under the age of 18 at the time of settlement, the settlement would have been made payable to her guardian. However, that was not the case with this situation. At the end, the person injured is who I represent. If you would like to further discuss your situation, please contact me directly at (954) 895-7455.

reviews.text: I strongly recommend Robert. An excellent experience from start to finish. Robert's knowledge and experience was extremely comforting and he took care of my case professionally. reviews.text:
Jeff Cecil and his Para legal assistant are the best in the business. I know Jeff a long time and he helped me navigate a complicated real estate transaction that required a return of funds on a purchase back in 2006. Jeff was very successful at getting my deposit returned to me. I could not be more pleased, Jeff has become a friend and mentor in the real estate business.

While I certainly don't know about every employee of this firm, I will say that they seem to primarily or heavily do work in in trust and estate planning. If you're looking for someone to take care of you and your money and ensure that you leave a legacy to your descendants, run - don't walk - to any other firm.
When my grandmother was in the hospital on her deathbed, 80 years old and dying of cancer, on so much medication that she probably had no idea what was happening, a lawyer from this firm was literally at her bedside, pushing a pen into her hand, pressuring her to sign. He convinced her that the best thing to do for her family was to appoint HIM as a trustee on all the accounts. Yes, you read that right! For nearly 30 years now, well AFTER he retired. As a matter of fact, he has been controlling the accounts, essentially putting our family through a situation like Britney Spears was in - needing to get approval for anything they wanted to spend money on - with money that should have been theirs. Badgering my poor elderly mother when she asked for funds (from HER own account) for a new car to the point where she just gave up. (Hers was 20 or more years old at the time.)
For some outrageous reason, Porter Wright allowed this lawyer to hand pick a few accounts and hold onto them as the trustee, even years after he retired and clearly wasn't of sound mind to continue in that capacity. Filed taxes late every year, causing undo fees and stress. Never available to assist with anything when needed, excuse after excuse. "The wheels of justice turn slowly" he'd say. Well, yes, when you refuse to reply to an email or phone call for weeks or months on end, dragging it out for that next quarterly payment and then the next, of course they do! Despicable.
True professionals, high end office

At this point, the lack of professionalism and communication has made us seriously consider seeking legal help elsewhere just to understand what is going on with the case. When only the best will do……

Unfortunately, divorces are a common byproduct of our modern living and blended extended families.  When I was served with divorce papers I was lucky to have an attorney recommend Lisa P. Kirby, in Naples, FL, to navigate me through the difficult legal process.

My divorce was complex in that it was a bicoastal filing in Florida while I resided in California.  We had properties in both states as well as a host of other legal issues that needed to be addressed.

Lisa’s incomparable knowledge of the family law courts is amazing.  She was able to outline the proper strategy with realistic expectations all while keeping me apprised of all the details throughout the preliminary stages.  This was especially important since I did not actually meet Lisa until the day before trial.

Lisa’s trial demeanor is brilliant as it reveled her character and integrity.  She skillfully covered each point and is well versed in case law, which she used throughout the trial.  However, we were wholly ruled against by the trial Judge, The Honorable Joseph G. Foster, and Lisa was quick to say that we should appeal to the Second District Court.

Thanks to her confidence and legal expertise we were successful in the Appellate Court with a reversal on a key property issue.   The resulting ruling,

Nelson v. Nelson, 189 So.3d 788 (Fla. Dist. Ct. App. 2016)

is now apart of the Florida Case Law and has been written up in dozens of legal publications.  This case is now used as the benchmark for family law and trust attorneys throughout the state.

This process took 2.5 years to complete.  I would not have traversed this legal maze successfully without the steadfast support of Lisa P. Kirby, the finest family law attorney in southern Florida.

Chris took me from the very beginnings, which both of us thought, would be easy and fairly quick, to trial. I do not live in Florida and that made choosing a Florida attorney a little more difficult.  A Florida family member had passed passed away late in 2014 and we needed to probate the Florida estate.  We ended up retaining Mr. Mast after speaking to a number of attorneys.  He was by far the most candid and most reasonable. The estate was not particularly complicated and Mr. Mast did not try to embellish or justify why he needed to charge an inflated fee.  His fee was most reasonable and his final bill was exactly where he projected it would be.  It was refreshing not to hear a "sales pitch" - a tactic that other florida attorneys used to try to justify why their fees should be highter.

reviews.text: Scott is an excellent attorney who is honest, direct, and principled in his approach. He communicates clearly and sets realistic expectations while advocating aggressively for his clients. If there are negative reviews, they likely come from opposing parties rather than dissatisfied clients. :)

This lawyer represents my husband. He has advised my husband to hide assets and not send my daughter to me with necessities such as her GLASSES. He calls me yelling at me and accusing me of being racist and other things. My husband abused the court system and not one thing has been done about it

This man is all about greed. He cares NOTHING about the well being of any child in a custody dispute. He helps his clients break laws, and causes nothing but chaos during a divorce proceeding. He is up all hours of the night sending threatening emails and thinking of new ways to write ridiculous requests for the other party involved. He brags about being friends with the judges here in Naples. He almost makes it sound as though the entire system is rigged, which sure feels like it here in Collier county. He has harassed his client Windell Ross' wife on multiple occasions, calling at early hours accusing her of being racist, and going to her probation officers office claiming she doesn't reside at her address when she has provided a LEASE. He will go out of his way to cause more trouble than is necessary. This lawyer needs to be investigated by the bar because I have no idea how he has made it this far. His "motions" are ridiculous and threatening. And he is getting away with entirely too much.

Terrible lawyer. He is helping his client Windell Ross break multiple laws. Taking a child across state line using a tro when the mother hasn't even resided in Florida for the past 8 months. No Naples judges are doing anything about it, and Mr Roth is calling the mother at late hours, accusing her of being racist and yelling at her on the top of his lungs. These things SHOULD NOT be acceptable.

This attorney does not care about your family and to resolve the case during a divorce, he will create unfair litigation and continue charging because that is all he cares about...money. I complaint to the Florida bar because he advised my former husband to disconnect the power during our divorce while I was living at the house with my children during a hot summer day. This man will not do what is right, he will use the law to attack you and will continue to take from his clients by extending litigation and not wanting to end cases, he will take your money and your children's money. Stay away, he has no morals and is very unethical.
Committed to a successful outcome.

If you need an attorney that will fight for you and stand up for what’s right- look no further. Scott is very passionate about making his case heard and will keep fighting for you until you win or get the results you are looking for. In my case he was understanding to the sensitive situation I was dealing with and offered me reasonable solutions that made sense. He was quick to defend me and did not let me settle when I felt like I was hitting a brick wall. My case was heard and my results couldn’t be better. He did an amazing job for me and I would highly recommend giving him a chance to work your case if you are looking for a game changer - he is the attorney for you! Thank you Scott.
He is an outstanding attorney wth a solid legal background and training.  He is a no nonsense person To get to the job right He is very direct to the point highly recommend it

Scott Roth has got to be the best practicing lawyer in Lee County!
He was referred to me by a past Judge, that explained if you want positive results this is your guy!! Obviously who referred him speaks volumes.
He takes the time to learn the scenario and with his vast experience he was able to glean the answers with questions and scenarios that helps you understand and comprehend the situation instantly.
Sometimes during stressful times like law suits you tend to not think clearly, trust me with Scott Roth's intensity and in my opinion brilliance he will keep you on track and feeling very secure he is taking care of you.
Scott handled a family case for me, and because he is a family man as well, he deeply understands the stress, the emotions and the psychological aspects of a case such as this.. and trust me when I say this....NO STONE WILL BE UN-TURNED WITH HIS LEGAL MIND!!!!
Nothing but Positive in my experience.
Scott was my divorce attorney. Very attentive and paid extreme attention to detail! So glad he was with me through that nightmare.
Very knowledgeable man. Helped me immensely with some of my family's legal issues. Would not be satisfied with my situation right now if it were not for Scott. Thank you.

Equally impressive is Jim’s level of professionalism and responsiveness. Communication is timely, questions are answered thoroughly and all interactions have reflected a genuine dedication to client service.

For anyone seeking top tier legal guidance in estate and tax planning, Jim’s firm sets the standard. Jim’s expertise, integrity and personalized approach make him a trusted partner for individuals and families looking to protect their legacy and plan confidently for the future.

I want to share with you an experience I had when I lost my husband. I was devastated and sad, not knowing what to do. There was money left in the bank with no beneficiary. I needed to find a lawyer, but I had never been in such a situation, dealing with legal matters. A friend recommended a lawyer to me, which turned out to be the worst thing that could have happened. At first, when I met her for the first time, she was nice, talked with me, and told me what I needed to do. I liked that she spoke Spanish as well.

Unfortunately, my husband's ex appeared and got a lawyer because she wanted the money my husband had left and also wanted me to repay money that had already been paid. My lawyer then told me she couldn't continue with the case and recommended her husband, who was a litigation attorney. That's when the process and my tragedy began, because this lawyer never explained anything to me or did anything. This process lasted almost three and a half years, and I do not recommend these lawyers at all to avoid what happened to me.

My lawyer was Christina Dawidow, and Joe Dawidow. Joe prolonged the case and I felt he did nothing for me. Christina is not a professional; she never spoke with me. When I wanted to talk to her, she had the paralegal talk to me instead. She never spoke with me directly. I give them a 000 rating. The reason I sought her out was because she spoke Spanish perfectly; her father was from El Salvador, and I thought she would understand me better. But it was the opposite; this lawyer was a disappointment. Joe was somewhat better but did not put effort into the matter, letting time pass by.
Christina is extremely knowledgeable and helped me make important decisions regarding my trust. They were very supportive and welcoming. I would recommend them for all your legal needs.
Great experience. We accomplished everything we needed. A very professional firm. The staff is efficient and gets back to you promptly.

I've been availing myself of Willis & Davidow services for several years now. They have come through every single time. Their legal knowledge and experience are second to none. reviews.text: Our organization has had the privilege of engaging Mr. Justin Bailey’s services over the course of several years. We have consistently found him to be highly professional, exceptionally knowledgeable, and thoroughly reliable, all while maintaining reasonable fee structures. On select occasions, we have also collaborated with Mr. John Goede, whose performance has been equally impressive and satisfactory. We wholeheartedly recommend this firm and continue to rely upon their expertise to fulfill all of our legal requirements.

While my case remains ongoing, I wish to commend Mr. John J. Truitt for the exceptional caliber of legal representation he has provided to date. His professionalism, intelligence, and steadfast sense of responsibility have been evident throughout every stage of the process.

Mr. Truitt has demonstrated a remarkable commitment to client communication. He consistently makes himself available to address my concerns, promptly schedules calls when needed, and—despite the demands of his schedule—never rushes a conversation. His ability to explain complex legal matters with clarity and patience has been both reassuring and invaluable.

It is evident that Mr. Truitt approaches his work with a high degree of integrity, diligence, and legal acumen. I have complete confidence in his guidance and strongly recommend him to anyone in need of a knowledgeable, responsive, and highly capable attorney.

Horrible experience, Never ever go this firm. They failed to put the correct context into important documents, which caused confusion, complications and disputes down the line. This lack of attention to detail led to a domino effect of issues that I had to deal with afterward—issues that could have been avoided with competent handling from the start.To make matters worse, I was hit with unexpected additional fees due to their own errors. Instead of taking accountability, they passed the cost on to me. Over time, staff kept changing, which meant I had to re-explain my case multiple times. It felt like I was starting over every few weeks. Most of the team was working remotely, and this really showed in the lack of cohesion, follow-up, and urgency.

Dealing with this firm was frustrating, stressful, and ultimately much more expensive than it should have been. I would not recommend them to anyone looking for reliable, professional legal representation.

Thank you so very much John Goede and assistant Stephanie. It was a lengthy process to close this deal. You were instrumental in making it happen! I am so relieved to have this closure. Whew⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️!

He takes the time to answer questions thoroughly, provides clear and actionable advice, and makes complex transactions feel manageable. I am writing this letter of recommendation on attorney Brandon Karas.  I live in Pittsburgh, PA and was in the middle of a real estate transaction that went south at the very end.  So much so that we had no choice but to hire an attorney to protect our family and investment from having hundreds of thousands of dollars taken from us.  I had less than 48 hours to respond to a letter from the opposition's attorney, so we had to ask our real estate agent if he knew anyone that he trusted.  He referred us to Brandon and we connected that day.  Brandon immediately got himself up to speed with the situation and worked with us to respond to the outrageous demand from the opposition.  We resolved the issue and the real estate transaction was saved.  Brandon was knowledgeable, responsive, transparent, and was extremely professional throughout the entire process.  I would highly recommend him and would use him again for any and all legal advice he holds an expertise in.  I will also be referring clients to him any time I hear about a need for his services.

Scott

reviews.text: UNETHICAL!   Pays for a service that calls people, UNSOLICITED, to ask if they need an attorney.  I got transferred all the way to intake in his office.   Look for more info in his Florida Bar Association listing and BBB.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We take allegations of unethical conduct very seriously. Our firm does not, and has never, engaged in unsolicited outreach in violation of ethical or legal standards, nor do we work with any third-party services that do so.
In fact, we have a recorded call in which you contacted our office directly and specifically stated you were seeking an attorney for an auto accident. This makes your claim that we reached out to you without cause extremely confusing and concerning.
If there has been any misunderstanding, we welcome the opportunity to clarify and resolve it. However, if this review was left in bad faith, or to benefit a competitor, particularly one you may be affiliated with, we will pursue all appropriate legal remedies.
Please feel free to reach out to our office directly at 239-500-5000 so we can address this properly.

reviews.text:
Find another personal injury attorney! There are thousands out there, and if you have a serious case, I recommend interviewing before ever signing anything with anyone. This firm has shown me that lawyers are the snakes they are often portrayed as.

I can tell you firsthand that this particular firm lacks communication, cohesiveness among one another, and the will to fight your case. They seem to have an extremely high turnover rate with their staff which also indicates internal issues galore.

If you have a SERIOUS, complex personal injury case that requires attention to detail and ability to communicate effectively with the client - run far away from this firm. In my case, this involved my minor (infant) child and using this firm was the biggest mistake I made in trying to help her. Do not let them tell you they are experienced in daycare cases, as they lied and told me. From start to finish it was a nightmare, including me having to coach my attorney into seeking discovery and refusing to let him talk me into settling at their first mediation and low ball offer. Then, they rushed into settlement as soon as they heard it hit my bare minimum, without explaining to me the process and despite my desire to take it to trial. It’s clear they just wanted a larger portion of the settlement without doing the work.

Because it’s was a minors case, I never formally approved the settlement and the lawyer simply petitioned directly to the judge after going quiet for more than a month - despite me hesitating and not being fully on board. Once that was in motion, it was nearly impossible to reverse.  They neglected to communicate what was happening, how the process worked, or whether I was truly on board with the settlement despite me consistently reaching out to make contact to discuss and ask about the progress of the case. At certain points they even lied to me about what was going on to cover up their own shortcomings (like forgetting to complete tasks they told me were already done). Lack of transparency from your representation is unacceptable.

From settlement on was the worst experience for 7 months of my life. Of course, initially it was “this will be done next week” (Dec 2024). After that, it was like they forgot I was a client at all and stopped working efficiently and closely on the case - which may seem fine in a regular personal injury case, but a minors personal injury case involves a LOT of after settlement work including a guardian ad litem, trusts, specialized bank accounts, and restrictions. None of which they could (or would) properly explain to me, or even how it all worked. As if it was the cherry on top, despite receiving the settlement it took them nearly a month to actually disburse the funds, once again neglecting to communicate with me when i called and emailed to find out where it was. Despite working with them for over a year, they never knew my daughter’s name (their client) and misspelled her name on multiple court documents as well as incorrectly listing her address which caused a number of issues. If they can’t get simple information in a computer system right, it should be no surprise they don’t remember the details and complex issues of particular cases.

Please do your research and choose a better lawyer for your case. If you have a car accident where you’re “injured”, then these ambulance chasers can maybe get the job done (but not without being in bed with the insurance company and coercing you into smaller settlement so they can avoid work). Otherwise, find a lawyer who actually cares because this firm doesn’t.

The tagline “big enough to handle serious cases, small enough to care” was a lie on both counts. Steer clear.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are disappointed to see this review, as it contains several misrepresentations of the work performed in your case. From the beginning, our leadership, including Marc Shapiro personally, was involved in addressing your concerns and guiding the process. This was a minor’s case, which means every step required strict court oversight and approval. Importantly, you formally approved the settlement, and it could not have moved forward without both your consent and the court’s approval.
While we understand this was a difficult and emotional matter, our team worked diligently and in full compliance with all legal requirements to secure a resolution in your child’s best interest. We remain proud of the work we do on behalf of injured clients and will continue to fight for them with integrity, transparency, and compassion. If you would like to discuss this further, please reach out to us directly at 239-500-5000.

reviews.text:
I will try to be objective in this review. Unfortunately, my experience with this law office was not very positive. During the handling of my case, they only contacted me to request accident documentation. Throughout the rest of my treatment for back and neck pain, I did not hear from them again—I was the one who had to call and ask about my case, as they never provided updates. The most surprising part was finding out during a medical appointment that my case had been closed. This information came from the front desk at the medical office, not from the law firm itself; they never called me to inform me about this development. To make matters worse, the front desk at the medical office was requiring me to pay out of pocket for the appointment right then, as they informed me that my case was no longer covered. Now, I am left with a poor settlement from the insurance company, ongoing back pain, and additional financial burdens for my treatment.

Base on my experience I do not recommend trusting your case to this law office, especially if you want the best for your health. They make you sign a document stating that you cannot reopen your case, which further limits your options. If you’re pursuing the best possible outcome for your well-being, I would advise looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse:

Carey drags things out to needlessly charge excessive billable hours. When we met for mediation he was totally unprepared, never read any of my emails I sent in preparation for mediation. I wouldn’t recommend him to my worst enemy.

Cary did a great job , he made me feel that he was looking out for me during the entire process.! Best divorce attorney in SWFL

I needed to gather my thoughts and honestly share my experience working with Cary Cliff. Let’s start with; Cary Cliff was the 3rd attorney I  hired. I had been in a divorce process for over 2 yrs with no children involved. My last attorney took complete advantage of me, over litigated my case, set me up for two mediations with no avail and depleted my savings account. I was lucky enough to have a family member step in and help me hire Cary Cliff who took my case a month before trial. He took one binder with him in the courtroom while my ex husbands attorney came in with 8. The man is an absolute beast in the court room and he means business. My case was settled in less than 4 hours, I didn’t even have to testify. He is worth so much more than he even knows. In a matter of a month, he won my case and made headway where no other attorney could in 2 yrs. He is worth every penny and then some. He is compassion, intelligent, witty and professional. If you want the job done right and save money in the long run… hire Carry Cliff, he does what no body else can, and I know it sounds cliche but he does it with a smile. When you speak with him, he makes you feel comfortable like you’re talking to a friend. He genuinely cares about his clients and their well being. He is a rock star in my book and I would not recommend anybody else. Remember me when you hire Carry Cliff, you won’t regret that decision if you want to win and settle your case.  I wish I would have know about him sooner, so I am giving you all a heads up!

To Cary Cliff- thank you for everything from the bottom of my heart, you have been a God send to so many of us

The attorney takes time with you to explain everything. Recommends very wise decisions. The office is inviting. All the workers were very very helpful. I'm so grateful. Accomplished everything I needed to do.

My 1st review was cut short. Cary Cliff has been very helpful in a difficult situation. Navigation of family law is very difficult for someone new to it. I have many friends who use Cary and trust him.
Wouldn’t know what to do without this kind, smart and thorough gentleman in my corner!

Professional, caring, and good at what he does. He works hard on behalf of his clients to help insure the best outcome. He won't sugarcoat it and while he will tell you honestly what your up against he still manages to keep you calm and confident when facing the courtroom with the judge and observers present.
He has been here for years..professional. Great group..

Cary is absolutely amazing. I had a very different, difficult, three year divorce. He was by my side every step of the way. He is who you want standing next to you in court and in mediation. I was able, with his help, to protect my daughter and get exactly what I felt was best. He didn't settle on any issue. The best.
Love Mr. Cliff and Amanda!
Amazing team of people dedicated to their clients and community! 5 stars are not enough. 🥳🙏🏼

Unfortunately my experience was anything but pleasant this morning with an attorney via a phone call.   I called the office last week to find out about amending something small in our will and asked for an email ahead of a call so that I would know if I would be charged for the call and  the charge to change the detail in the will that this same attorney drew up for us a few years ago. I never received an email ahead of time but I did receive a call from the attorney at our scheduled time.  He said he had a note to call me at this time and I explained that I was hoping to receive an email ahead of time regarding any fees for his time.  He did hesitantly say he would not charge me for the call but he also annoyingly said  - everyone has to make a living so get on with it, what do you need-.  I was obviously taken aback by his curt reply.  I then explained that this is exactly why I asked for an email for clarification prior to a call so that I would not take any of his time if I was made aware of a fees, as per my request, last week.  Maybe he was having a bad day, but everyone’s  time is valuable, there is no hierarchy when it comes to treating others with respect.
Great team and I have had a great experience with multiple attorneys in multiple different fields of expertise.  Great people that aren't looking at the clock every hour, they just help
Spectacular food. Huge portions. Very reasonable prices. Checks all the boxes.
One of my 'go-to' law firms for real estate closings.

Top quality service , Quick and Very professional

Last year my husband’s passport was stolen. Since he was not born in America, other than his passport that had been stolen, he had no other way to provide proof of U.S. citizenship because a N-600 (proof of citizenship) was never filed on his behalf. After researching what his necessary steps should be in July of 2023 we filed and submitted the necessary documents for him to obtain his proof of citizenship. Once we filed for the N-600 and received the receipt number we have not heard back from the USCIS for over 6 months.  We made several attempts to contact the USCIS to only get even more discouraged because we were not able to get in contact with anyone.  After feeling defeated, I googled several immigration attorneys around my area to only discover that no one would even talk to me without a consultation fee.  However, Marcus Wobschall, Esq. from the Veritas Immigration Law, P.A. returned my voicemail within a few hours and spoke with me for over two hours regarding the difficult position my husband is in and the strain it is putting on me and my family. Marcus earnestly told me what my options were and candidly disclosed what he could do for me.  Within a month of retainment, my husband received his Biometrics appointment and will take his Oath of Allegiance at the end of this month! I am very happy with Marcus representation and I am glad I chose him for representation other than all of the other Immigration lawyers out there. Highly recommend!!!

reviews.text: As my parents aged, this team patiently guided us through.

I visited Burzynski Elder Law after work to talk about estate planning with a focus on long‑term care. Their team listened patiently and guided me through the options for elder law and asset protection without making me feel rushed. I felt confident choosing them as my estate planning attorney.
I came in kinda nervous for my mom stuff and they really helped me lot. Nice people patient and got things moving fast.

My husband and I were given the recommendation to call Burzynski Elder Law Firm.  We have had the immense privilege of working with Dan, Sue, Pieri, and Andrea. They have been a tremendous help with my mom who has Alzheimer’s Disease and my mentally handicapped sister. We did not know where to began- my mom and sister did not have Medicaid. After our first meeting with Dan and Sue, they set a plan in motion and have stayed on course to accomplish every goal that was made. Dan and Andrea have helped with applying for VA benefits for my sister since my Dad served in the Air Force during Vietnam. Sue and Pieri have helped with the Medicaid process and setting up a Trust for them. Sue found placement for them to stay together at a beautiful facility in Naples. She is an incredible advocate for us, and I know she cares with all her heart. It was the best decision to call them, and we are grateful for all their work and support. We will continue using this Law firm and highly recommend them to others.
Very attentive and compassionate people
Jill, Pieri, Sue and Andrea are phenomenal at what they do. You wont find a better elder care / life care planning law firm in Naples!

reviews.text: Not professional

08/23 update, I noticed that the original review was deleted again so I'm reposting it. No condolences, no apologies for their behavior was ever received.

Our experience with Burzynski Elder Care was horrendous.  The stress they put my 80 year old father throughout the ordeal was excruciating.  We are a small family, our priority was caring for my sick mother and having common legal documents done.  Dan Burzynski changed the legal documents after it was already approved by the entire family.  Burzynski confirmed everything prior to the final copy but then changed it to his own personal preference.  An attorney can advise and recommend but they can not alter legal documents against the clients direction.  Dan Burzynski refused to correct his problem and then decided to terminate a 1 year contract after only 2 months of wasting our time.  They refused any refund.  My father paid them $18,000 for this, 2 months of difficulty.  It was as if we hired a firm to fight against us.  My mother passed away in January, I miss her very much.  If she knew how our family was mistreated she would have been furious.
After the Burzynski relationship ceased I handled all of the responsibilities as her health care surrogate. I dealt directly with the nursing home, doctors, insurance, etc. The day to day ran smoother without Burzynski's involvement.  This firm was aware of my mother's deteriorating condition yet they still treated my father with zero respect, it was disgusting. Normally when you pay inflated fees you get exceptional service, not the case here. Instead we experienced arrogance and laziness.  Dan Burzynski repeatedly lied about my father and I.  We were dealing with a firm that refused to complete the simplest task.  We expected some guidance on estate planning, none was given. Two of the three legal documents we needed were done incorrectly.
Money was never a question with our family.  The last Will and Testament was completed over twenty years ago and all the beneficiary's were already set.  All of the legal forms and problems that we had with this firm have since been corrected but it doesn't take take away the pain and anguish we experienced during their short involvement with my family.
Twenty years ago, we lived through a similar situation when my grandparents became ill.  We got the necessary healthcare and nursing assistance, no attorney was ever needed nor hired and we got through that rough time without the drama of this experience.
My main concern now is looking after my father.  What can be done to make the Burzynski mess better?  My father and I would like the Burzynski Elder Care Firm to donate the $18,000 that we gave to them to the Cancer Research Institute or the Fisher Center for Alzheimer Research Foundation in the name of my mother, Liliane.
reviews.ownerResponse: A lawyer’s duty to keep client confidences has few exceptions and in an abundance of caution I do not feel at liberty to respond in a point by point fashion in this forum. Mom loved Sue...and after she passed all the paperwork  and documents were all taken care of and it is flowing seamlessly so far.
My wife and I and my brother are now also clients of Burzynsky
Elder Law.
Thank you all for your care!
Dave Burgham

reviews.text:
My family has been affiliated with Jill and Dan 's firm for many years.  When taking care of my father, my mother came to them for relief.  They met with mom and created a plan.  The biggest hurdle was navigating the difficult path of being admitted to a Veteran's hospital.  Dan and the staff took care of all of the details.  This was a game changer for my mother:  the perfect solution for dad, who  was receiving superb care and mom could focus on her own health needs.
When dad did pass away, Dan and Jill were right there to help with the details.  Thanks to their prior planning, there were no surprises.
When mom decided to downsize, they were there helping  with many aspects of the real estate transaction,  over seeing her  financial planning and mapping out her own future.
For her children, we were comforted knowing that mom's needs were being guided by a group of people who truly were looking out for her best interest.  Now that mom has passed away, they continue to help us during this difficult time.  Dan and Jill have been gently guiding us all for many years.   They welcome us not as clients, but as people who have a need, for which they  provide solutions.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Susan. It has been our pleasure to work with your family.

reviews.text:
I needed a review, interpretation and guidance regarding a document in a mortgage package from my separated wife. I needed to ensure I wasn’t being snowballed into financial obligations for her mortgage. I contacted Mathew Goodwin at less than 36 hours prior to signing due to the mortgage company dragging their feet in providing documents to me. Mathew Goodwin’s office staff understood my predicament and was very reassuring all would be okay.

Mathew Goodwin called me with a full understanding of my situation and needs. He provided line-by-line interpretation and assurance I could sign the document with no fear of any present nor future financial/legal responsibilities. He also invited me to call him at the signing if I had questions.

At the signing, five more documents arose for my signature. Three were in question. Matthew Goodwin returned my call within minutes, explained the three docs and assured me they were harmless to sign.

Thanks to Mathew Goodwin for easing my mind during a very questionable and tumultuous 36 hours.

I want to express my sincere gratitude to The Law Offices of Neil Morales, P.A. for their outstanding work during my bankruptcy proceedings. I came to their office during one of the most difficult times of my life, carrying an emotional and financial burden that seemed impossible to manage. From day one, attorney Neil Morales provided me with clarity, confidence, and a solid plan to regain my stability.

His professionalism is impeccable: he explains each step with admirable patience, answers all questions honestly, and never makes you feel judged for your situation. On the contrary, he treats you with dignity and respect, as someone who genuinely wants to help you move forward.

I want to especially acknowledge the work of his assistant, Maharai Dacosta, FRP, whose support was crucial throughout the entire process. Maharai was always attentive, organized, and willing to guide me with kindness. Her efficiency and compassionate approach made all the difference.

Thanks to them, the process was much simpler and less stressful than I had imagined. Today, I can say that I have regained my peace of mind and a new opportunity to start over.

I recommend The Law Offices of Neil Morales, P.A. with complete confidence. If you are looking for a professional, honest, efficient team that is truly committed to its clients, this is the right place.
I want to share my deepest appreciation for The Law Offices of Neil Morales, P.A. for their exceptional work during my bankruptcy process. I walked into their office during one of the most stressful and overwhelming moments of my life. Attorney Neil Morales provided clarity, reassurance, and a solid plan that helped me move forward with confidence.

His professionalism is outstanding. He explains every step with patience, honesty, and respect, making sure you fully understand your options. At no point did I feel judged or embarrassed—on the contrary, he treated me with dignity and made me feel supported throughout the entire process.

I also want to highlight the incredible work of his assistant, Maharai Dacosta, FRP. She was attentive, organized, and absolutely essential in guiding me through all the paperwork and requirements. Her kindness and efficiency made the process much smoother.

Thanks to this team, what I expected to be a painful and complicated experience turned into a manageable and even hopeful one. Today I can say I have peace of mind again and a fresh start.

I highly recommend The Law Offices of Neil Morales, P.A. If you are looking for a professional, honest, efficient, and genuinely caring team, this is the place you can trust.

I sent her all the pictures of my bruises the bump on my chest from fractured sternum.  Everything was done through video conversations with Bruce Scheiner. He explained the process and had compassion for the situation. They all became like my extended family. After awhile I was assigned to Dan Delasandro. He also was so kind and worked hard on my case. Best criminal Attorney in south Florida. You get what you pay for

Jerry has been practicing law for over 35 years.  There is no substitute for that breadth of experience.  He is also a Board Certified Criminal Defense lawyer.  The majority of those who practice criminal law do not take the time or expend the effort to become "board certified."  Jerry employs an extraordinarily talented team, including a knowledgeable, former DMV manager, who assists clients with drivers license issues that result from criminal charges.  Jerry is thorough, yet efficient.  He takes his time with clients and makes their concerns a top priority.  He is committed to practicing ethically and treating his clients with dignity and respect.

For me, being arrested was a difficult and trying time.  I was embarrassed and "shopped" several criminal defense lawyers before choosing Jerry.  I can say without hesitation choosing Jerry (and his team) was the right decision.  In addition to the amazing legal talent that comes with hiring the Law Offices of Jerry Berry, I never felt judged or condemned for my mistake.  Jerry was honest and upfront with me, while treating me with dignity and respect.  I was pleased with result of my case, but more pleased with how I was treated.  Unlike other Naples lawyers, during the initial consultation I never felt like he was trying to "sell" me.  He doesn’t make empty promises and he takes his time explaining everything.  My concerns were addressed thoroughly and with the utmost competence.
Great lawyer. Very thorough and utilizes time efficiently. Respectful through the whole process.

I can testify to the fact that Jerry is the type of lawyer every client wants, and in my opinion other lawyers hope to be. He has the utmost respect and admiration of clients, fellow attorneys, and judges here in the Naples area. If you need the very best defense possible I recommend that he is the one to call in the Naples area.

Joseph Coleman represented me in a building contractor fraud case in Florida. He was successful, and I received a civil judgement in the case, It took the State of Florida over a year to bring the criminal case. Due to high level of contractor fraud in Florida. I recently heard back from the State Attorneys Office, that they are negotiating a full payment plan, to fully refund my payment. I know , had Mr Coleman not obtained a civil judgement, that I would not have received any money back, and the contractor would have continued stealing from innocent citizens. I would highly recommend Mr Coleman, and his very professional law office. His staff kept me fully updated during the entire process.

She did an outstanding job, and we settled for six figures, something I never thought we would even get near to. But with excellent legal knowledge and professionalism Ciara got the job done. She believes in personal/transparent contact with her clients, not turning the client towards the office staff. Although the office staff plays an integral part in her practice, she really does converse with the client, which is what I like most about her. I can’t praise or recommend her enough. She is the go to attorney for Southwest Florida, and I’m confident that you will agree.
Even when I’ve just had a quick legal question, Ciara has always taken the time to help. She’s professional, sharp, and truly one of the best attorneys I know. I Highly recommend her.

The absolute best human (Ciara) you could ask for, when it matters the most. She’s my go to, when I need the best advice.

Absolutely would recommend.

I wholeheartedly recommend Gulf Coast Law PA and their firm to anyone looking for a trustworthy and exceptional estate attorney. They truly go above and beyond!

Ciara is the best!! Very reliable and responsive to all of my questions or concerns. She has been a huge help with making sure everything is completed in a timely manner, I refer her to all my friends and family!

reviews.text:
# Outstanding Estate Planning Services - Highly Recommend Gulf Coast Law PA

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5 stars)

I cannot speak highly enough about my experience with Gulf Coast Law PA for my estate planning needs. From start to finish, the entire process was handled with exceptional professionalism and expertise.

What impressed me most was how thoroughly Lister, explained each aspect of estate planning in terms I could actually understand. Legal documents can be intimidating, but she took the time to walk me through every detail, ensuring I felt confident about each decision. Her knowledge of estate law is clearly extensive, and she provided valuable insights I hadn't considered.

If you're looking for estate planning services, I wholeheartedly recommend Gulf Coast Law PA. The peace of mind that comes from having your affairs properly organized is invaluable, and you couldn't ask for better guidance through the process.

**Date of Service:** 9/17/2024
**Services Used:** Estate Planning
Always a pleasure to work with!

I'm writing to express my sincere satisfaction with the work of Ciara, who recently assisted with a contract issue between a buyer and seller I was representing.

Ciara's prompt and clear guidance on the matter was invaluable. She was able to identify a solution that effectively addressed the concerns of both parties, leading to a positive outcome for everyone involved.

We were all very impressed with Ciara's expertise and professionalism, and I would highly recommend her to anyone seeking assistance with their real estate needs.
Prompt, professional, and made me feel secure with every step of the process.
Thank you Ciara for your legal advice.  Simply the best!

While I haven’t personally worked with Ciara Lister, her strong local reputation and praise from mutual connections make her someone I’d confidently recommend. She’s known in the Southwest Florida community for her professionalism, responsiveness, and genuine care for her clients. If you’re looking for a trustworthy and knowledgeable attorney, Ciara is highly regarded and would be a great person to call!
Ciara was very knowledgeable and helpful in assisting me with my estate planning.

Ciara is truly great at what she does! It’s really easy to feel impersonal and “just a number” when they deal with the same things each day. Ciara adds the personal touch, and hears you out to fully understand the situation, and give you the best help she can provide!

Not everyone’s experiences are the same, and she made sure I felt heard. There was little to no confusion in the process because she took the time at the beginning to understand right where I was at!
Clearly cares about his clients and very easy firm to work with.

I have known Tom for a number of years and highly endorse him as an attorney. He is a skilled advocate on behalf of his clients and is very knowledgeable. He really cares about his clients and takes the time to do things the right way!
Jim is absolutely the BEST!  He goes out of his way to service his clients.  Litigation can be stressful, and he always helps in a bind!

Absolutely outstanding. I have trusted my firm's printing needs to Jim Yoder and Steel Litigation for years, and he never fails to go above and beyond to get the job done. Jim has handled my trial notebooks, bench books, and even last minute weekend emergency print jobs, Jim always does an exceptional job and offers ideas on how to make the final product as user friendly as possible. I would not trust anyone else with my firm's printing needs.

Going through arguably the most difficult time of my life, I was fortunate enough to have Attorney Josh Faett by my side. From day one, Josh consistently showed me—truthfully and honestly—that there was light at the end of the tunnel. The process was long and challenging, but if you put your faith and trust in what Josh says, he will lead you down the right path.

I cannot begin to describe how grateful I am for Josh and Jacqueline at The Faett Firm. These are two people who genuinely care about doing the right thing and helping you work toward a realistic and fair outcome. Josh never skirts around the truth—he’s straightforward and honest with you, whether it’s what you want to hear or not—and that level of integrity is rare.

If you or anyone you know ever needs an attorney who will fight, advocate, and work tirelessly for you, I cannot recommend The Faett Firm enough. They are truly lifesavers, and I will forever be grateful for everything they’ve done for me.

I can’t say enough about Attorney Josh Faett and his fantastic assistant Jacqueline. For over two years, they guided our family through one of the most challenging times of our lives with compassion, professionalism, and constant support.

Josh’s legal expertise and knowledge of complex law are extraordinary. His competence and strategic thinking gave us hope when we needed it most. He fought relentlessly for my fiancé and ultimately saved his life, giving him a second chance and our family peace again. His dedication, integrity, and competence were evident in every step of the case.

Jacqueline was equally incredible, caring, organized, and always communicative. She made sure we understood everything and always treated us with kindness and respect.

Together, Josh and Jacqueline carried our family through a storm with strength and compassion. We will be forever grateful for how much they cared and how hard they worked for us. If you need a team that truly fights for you and believes in you, Josh and Jacqueline are the ones to trust.

Responsive, knowledgeable, and very easy to work with. The Faett Firm was great. Josh Faett took care of both the legal and emotional aspect’s of my case. His paralegal Jackie went above and beyond to help me with any questions I had. I would highly recommend working with the Faett Firm.
Wonderful attorney!

I had 3 charges and my bond was set at over 1 million dollars. I was facing up to 35 years in prison. Josh got me the deal of a lifetime without selling my soul. I ended up with a plea deal of 6 years probation without my "cooperation". Josh is unbelievable and is a man of his word. I highly recommend him to anyone with criminal charges.
Jesus B

I highly recommend josh to anyone who has any legal trouble going on. There were many aspects of my case that weren’t in my favor, and the prosecutors did not want to cut me any sort of deal. We ended up having to take the case to trial after 7 months of going back and forth, during this time josh kept well in the loop and he was very straightforward with me about all the outcomes. During the trial is where i really saw what type of lawyer Josh is. I sat there for 4 hours getting the finger pointed at me by the prosecutors and I was looking pretty guilty. Then Josh started flipping the script entirely, he started questioning the police officer and the prosecutions case started falling apart. He was really in his element in that courtroom you could tell this wasn’t his first time around. After being in that courtroom for 8 hours we left winning the entire case. Josh exceeded my expectations by far, hands down one of the best lawyers ive ever worked with. I would recommend him to any of my friends and family going through any legal trouble.

There are those who settle and take your money for a quick profit and whatever deal they are given, then there are those like Mr. Faett, he doesn’t settle and goes for the best possible outcome, I put my full trust into him and am very glad I did, got my charges dismissed and every step of the way was honest with me, he knows what he is doing and a upon my calendar hearing it gave me a much better perspective, the deals he got, and outcomes thoroughly surpassed everyone else’s in the courtroom, I am extremely impressed with his consistent results not with just myself, if I know anyone who gets in trouble they will be referred to him, there is no substitute for a lawyer that leaves such a imprint of when the prosecutor reads the defense attorneys name, they have to change how they want to approach due to knowing he isn’t going to be a push over and get the best outcome in favor of his client. Thank you Mr. Faett for a phenomenal outcome and justifying my trust being put into you.
never contact me in jail and doesn’t fight for you not worth the money
Joshua Faett has helped my family on many fronts….he has always come through for us.  If you need help you should call Joshua and the Faett Firm.

Josh has been my attorney for over 20yrs. Very knowledgeable and a straight shooter.  I've always listened to his advice which has kept me out of trouble. I recommend him to all may family and friends.

Highly recommended

Attorney Joshua Faett and his assistant Jacquelyn are absolutely AMAZING!!!  If I could award more than 5 stars I would.  A family member was facing some serious felony charges with a minimum mandatory prison sentence in excess of 15 years and ended up getting 5 years probation.  Attorney Faett had a plan and laid out a path, and over the course of approximately 21 months everything fell into place.  He tells it like it is and he does not sugar-coat anything and I like that.  He treated us like family and never gave up, even when dealing with such a difficult case.  The State Prosecutor was doing their best to make a case, but Attorney Faett was able to turn it around with facts and convince the court otherwise.  I cannot thank him enough for keeping our family member in "the sunshine."  We even received feedback from others in the field of law during the case and they advised Attorney Faett was very well-respected in the court system and that we had made a good chose when hiring him to defend the case.  That really put our minds at ease knowing we had chosen the right Attorney for the job.  Please consider Attorney Faett if you need a defense attorney...you will not be disappointed!!!

5 stars for the Faett Firm. If you’re looking for legal council this is the place. Josh and his assistant Jackie are always on top of everything and communicate efficiently! We’ve given him our trust throughout and in return he’s given us his all! Thank you! 🙏🏼

reviews.text: My husband & I HIGHLY recommend Josh if you're looking for an attorney. He's straight forward & very down to earth. He went above & beyond to help us with our case. Jackie, his assistant was extremely helpful as well. Look no further if you're in need of an attorney, Josh is awesome!!
Best lawyer in town, if your in trouble call him he'll take care of you.

Josh came referred to me from numerous people.  When I first met him and his assistant Jackie,  he was as straight forward as one can be.  Basically telling me what I needed to hear, not what I wanted to hear.  Nothing was sugar coated or downplayed.  It was reminder throughout the entire process of how serious this was.  He told me from the beginning that this was a case that would come down to the wire.  That was an understatement.  Josh had a game plan that he wanted to do and his judgment for this plan was a life saver.  When he said down to the wire, I never expected the final few days.  The legal process is stressful and for those unfamiliar with the process it can be very strangulating on your life, your thoughts and sleep.  Both Josh's and Jackie's demeaner is calming and definitely helped take the edge off when talking with them. Getting in touch with Jackie and Josh was very easy and usually with a quick call back if needed.  If there was any advice that I could give, it would be to have patience and trust in that Josh is very much aware of what needs and can be done.  I owe Josh a great deal of gratitude for saving my freedom and will forever be thankful.

If your reading reviews looking for the perfect attorney look no further. Attorney Josh and his team are a life saver. I would 10 of 10 recommend Josh for counsel.

I had josh on a DUI case he advised me that if i took the probation i would be able to get out of jail and be able to move because my family was relocating we asked josh if we should wait or if they could just move he advised that it was no problem they should go on ahead and when I got out I could just report to probation and tell them I was leaving when I got to probation they advised me that I was going nowhere and that my lawyer was not correct I ended up being homeless no job and on intensive probation for a year he assured my family that it was going to be no issue it was a huge issue just felt other people thinking of using him should know!!! Leslie knew the medical terms and the different conditions that I needed to claim. She made it simple for me to understand.  Being a Vet herself...she gets it.

On behalf of my family and myself.... Thank you Leslie! You and your Firm have not only helped me but my family and their future in these tough times.

Now let me end this before it somehow erases again and I have to start all over.

P.S. Already got my battle buddy his bottle.

Deans practice and office are beyond horrible. I signed up for their waitlist back in April. Said it could be a while which I was ok with. I was receiving emails about my status on the waitlist; on June 18 “you’re number 52” and on July 16 “you are number 18 and you should be contacted in approximately 2 weeks”. I was not contacted in two weeks. When I called to inquire they said unfortunately we had to terminate the list. How does a waitlist get terminated? You serve the next client in line. They would not tell me why. I told them I know this process takes a while but I had till December to get my paperwork in (getting appointments and the VA process takes forever as we all know). They said it wouldn’t be a problem and they would get me in before then. I paid the fee and signed the limited power of attorney. Once they received my VA file they scheduled me for a consult in January 2026. When I asked why it wasn’t before Dec like they had said, they replied with we didn’t say that. YES THEY DID. After this I contacted other firms to see how they operate and every firm I spoke with said that they are not allowed to charge me any fees other than the 20% of retro pay. Dean Law Firm charges a $2500 processing fee plus 20% of retro pay. I have no idea if that is true or not however 4 other firms said it’s not allowed. It is clear since they had me file my intent with the VA in April after I first contacted them, they pushed my case back as far as possible in order to receive maximum back pay from my case. They are only concerned with your money and not you as a veteran.
reviews.ownerResponse: We hate to hear this from you. We have a long wait list, and at the onset we let prospective clients know this who want pre-filing consult work (which we can charge for/searching for new items that may present and digging through records and other elements which takes a great deal of time). We do not charge to file new claims and appeals/supplementals/20% contingency is standard. Any money you paid for PFC work that you didn't want to pursue because it wasn't to your time preference was refunded in full. Good luck to you.

Concerns Regarding Leslie Dean Law Firm's Handling of My MST Case..I'm deeply concerned about the Leslie Dean Law Firm's sudden reversal on my Military Sexual Trauma (MST) case, especially given the strong initial assessment and the sensitive nature of the documentation I provided.

As a documented MST survivor, I experienced severe harm on November 22, 1989, aboard the USS Abraham Lincoln CVN 72, at the hands of seven sailors, including two First Classes and three officers. This trauma is officially documented in my ship's medical file, recorded just minutes after the assault. Despite receiving an Honorable Discharge, my DD-214 initially cited "personality disorder" as the reason, a designation that essentially implied inconvenience to the federal government. While the Board of Correction for Military Records did change the name on my DD-214 last year, it was reclassified under "secretarial authority," which carries the same implication of inconvenience.

Melving, an associate at Leslie Dean Law Firm, explicitly stated that my case had "very strong merit." Based on this assurance, I authorized the release of all my VA medical records to their firm. Shockingly, just seven days later, they rejected my claim. Legal professionals I've consulted have expressed that such a rapid and abrupt change in assessment, especially after receiving sensitive documents from an MST survivor, is highly unusual.

While Leslie Dean Law Firm addressed the document issue and their withdrawal process, they have yet to provide a clear, detailed explanation for their initial assessment of "strong merit" followed by their sudden decision to decline my case, particularly concerning my retroactive compensation. This lack of transparency, compounded by the use of exclamation points in their follow-up emails, raises significant red flags regarding their professional communication and practices.

I'm left feeling incredibly saddened and frustrated by what appears to be another instance of encountering dishonest legal services. Bill Apgar Dot Com
reviews.ownerResponse: After a courtesy review of records and filings to make a determination of client engagement, our firm made the decision to not accept veteran's case.  Taking a fee and retainer to perform work that would most likely be unsuccessful is not in the best interest of the veteran and our firm.  We're sorry that Mr. Apgar doesn't agree with our decision, we wish him well.

reviews.text:
I retired from the U.S. Army at the end of 2006, and filed for VA Disability and Reevaluations a or eight times between 2007 and 2024. I filed myself several times, and then filed through the Disabled American Veterans (DAV) as my advocate. No matter how much evidence upon evidence and years of follow up doctor visits was presented, there seemed to be no movement from a 90% rating to a 100% rating. A friend recommended I hire the Leslie Dean Law Office as my advocate next time. I called and spoke with Leslie, and immediately knew they meant business and stand up for Disabled Veterans. I signed on as a client right away. Within a few months my name came to the top of the list, and Leslie and her team had my packet ready to submit. I was scheduled to be seen by a number of doctors over the following six or seven months and then the VA processed their decision... 100% Total and Permanent Disabled, and back dated the effective date by nearly two years! I refer all Veterans I know to Leslie Dean and her team of professionals now, and am so very thankful to them for acting as my advocate through this submittal! Give Leslie a call and at least hear what her team can offer .. you will be thankful you did.

Clayton Wey
MSG - U.S. Army - Retired

reviews.text:
Unprofessional and Misleading - Our Family’s Life Put on Hold

As the husband of the O1 visa applicant, I’m writing to express our deep disappointment with this immigration lawyer. From the beginning, he assured us that my wife had more than enough qualifications to secure the visa. Trusting his judgment, we paid his high fees and waited over a year and a half with very little communication or updates. We eventually had to pay for premium processing to speed things up—despite the fact that this visa process usually takes about two months.

Even after all that time, USCIS requested more documents, which should have been properly submitted from the start. After months of waiting, our application was denied, with USCIS stating that we didn’t meet any of the requirements and that the submission was poorly handled.

This lawyer gave us false hopes, leading us to believe everything was under control while our entire life, and the lives of our children, were put on hold for almost two years. His poor handling of the case and lack of professionalism were incredibly frustrating. I would not recommend his services to anyone in need of reliable immigration assistance.
reviews.ownerResponse:
While we understand your disappointment with the results of your wife's case with USCIS, your review can be interpreted as misleading.

When initially contacted, you were advised you met the eligibility requirements for the O-1 visa application. However, at no point was any guarantee given that your case would be approved as this is completely out of our control. Same with the processing times. The choice for premium processing was always there from the beginning but not required and was an option always given. The handling of the review of the case is not within a lawyer's control, but that of USCIS and the officer reviewing the case.

USCIS is free to send Requests for Evidence at any point of a case. That does not mean that the documents originally submitted were improperly submitted.

Our office has had other successful O-1 approvals and unfortunately, your case was not one of those. After receiving the denial, you were contacted and explained of your options. Your assertion that work was done incorrectly or that there was no communication just because of a negative result is unwarranted.

reviews.text:
Terrible Experience - Misleading Guidance, False Hopes, and Visa Denied

I had an extremely disappointing experience with this immigration lawyer while applying for my O1 visa. He assured me from the start that I had more than enough qualifications to obtain the visa. After paying his expensive fees and waiting for over a year and a half with almost no updates, I was forced to pay additional premium processing fees just to speed up the process—when typically, this type of visa only takes about two months to process.

After all that, USCIS requested more documents, which should have been properly submitted in the first place. After a couple more months of waiting, my visa application was denied, with USCIS stating that we did not meet any of the requirements and that the files were improperly submitted.

This lawyer not only gave us false hopes but also kept my life—and the lives of my children—on hold for all this time. I trusted his supposed expertise, but it’s clear that my case was completely mismanaged. I would strongly advise against hiring him for any immigration matters.
reviews.ownerResponse:

Our office has had other successful O-1 approvals and unfortunately, your case was not one of those. After receiving the denial, you were contacted and explained of your options. Your assertion that work was done incorrectly or that there was no communication just because of a negative result is unwarranted.

reviews.text: They asked me for 500$ just for consultation !!! This is ridiculous 😒
reviews.ownerResponse: $500 for an initial consultation with an AV rated litigator as well with market prices. I’m sorry if you were hoping for a free consultation. Our attorneys have to make a living, providing legal guidance to people. In many instances, the first hour of legal advice is the most valuable because the attorney can advise whether or not you actually have a good case and or help you with self-help ideas.  In some cases, we do provide free consultations, personal injury, for example, but only when the underlying matter is a contingency case.  If we offered free consultations with standard litigation cases, that’s all we would do all day. And in many instances, all we do during a initial consultation is try to talk people out of commercial litigation because it is so inefficient and so uncertain. As you can imagine, that’s not a very good way to make a living.

What impacted me most was feeling her sincerity and her heart. Not only did she guide me legally, she also gave me advice from faith and from God, and that touched me deeply.
